text:
Market today:
Geopolitical tensions are flaring with U.S. strikes on Iran, pushing oil prices higher and stoking global risk aversion. For your tech/chip heavy book, this means increased volatility and potential for risk-off sentiment. China-related tech news (Apple testing chips, AI probes) also adds a layer of uncertainty.

Your holdings:
SOXL (-35.06%) is a major concern; its leveraged nature means deep losses are amplified, especially on margin. SPAL (-23.1%) is also a new, leveraged position that demands immediate attention. QQQI (-2.16%) is flagged for a trim by your scanner, with its stop at 52.

Watch:
Keep a close eye on geopolitical developments and oil prices, as they will dictate broader market sentiment. Also, monitor any further news on China's role in the tech sector, particularly regarding Apple and AI.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management and protect capital in this uncertain environment.
plays: Consider exiting or significantly reducing SOXL exposure given its deep loss and leveraged decay, amplified by margin.
Watch SPAL closely; its -23.1% loss on a new, leveraged position demands immediate attention. Respect its stop at 21.38.
Trim QQQI to respect its scanner status and stop at 52, reducing your income anchor exposure.
